byok.title: About BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
byok.description: AI Studio uses a BYOK model where you provide your own API keys. This gives you full control over cost and usage. Your keys are stored encrypted and used only for your requests.
byok.anthropic: Best for complex training programs and reasoning
byok.google: Best for video analysis and multimodal input
byok.openai: Used for embeddings and document search

changePlan.question: Can I change my plan at any time?
changePlan.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. When upgrading, you get access to new features immediately. When downgrading, the new plan takes effect from the next billing period.

athleteLimit.question: What happens if I have more athletes than my plan allows?
athleteLimit.answer: You keep access to all existing athletes, but you cannot add new ones until you either upgrade or remove some athletes.

payment.question: How does payment work?
payment.answer: We use Stripe for secure payments. You can pay by card and will be billed automatically every month or year depending on your chosen billing period.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits your needs

trial.question: Is there a free trial?
trial.answer: Yes! The Starter plan is completely free and you can use it as long as you want with up to 2 athletes.

changePlan.question: Can I change my plan at any time?
changePlan.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. When upgrading, you get access to new features immediately.

payment.question: What payment methods are accepted?
payment.answer: We accept all major credit cards via Stripe. Invoicing is also available for Business and Enterprise plans.

cancelAnytime.question: Can I cancel at any time?
cancelAnytime.answer: Yes, you can cancel your subscription at any time. You keep access to the service until the end of your billing period.

hero.title: Run faster with
hero.description: Combine scientific precision with modern technology. Get tailored training programs based on your lactate tests, heart rate zones and daily readiness.

features.title: Everything you need to improve
features.description: From advanced tests to daily follow-up – we give you the tools that elite athletes use.

dynamicPrograms.title: Dynamic Programs
dynamicPrograms.description: Training programs that adapt to your progress and your daily readiness.

lactateZones.title: Lactate & Zones
lactateZones.description: Exact training zones based on your lactate tests for optimal intensity control.

readinessHrv.title: Readiness & HRV
readinessHrv.description: Daily health check that adjusts today's workout based on your recovery.

racePrediction.title: Race Prediction
racePrediction.description: See what you can achieve in the 10K, half marathon or marathon based on your data.

deepAnalysis.title: Deep Analysis
deepAnalysis.description: Track your progress over time with clear graphs and trends.

smartCalendar.title: Smart Calendar
smartCalendar.description: Plan your season and peak your fitness exactly when it matters.

science.title: Built on science, not guesswork.
science.description: We use proven methods to ensure every step counts. By basing your training on actual physiological data, you minimize injury risk and maximize results.
science.step1Description: Perform lactate or field tests to find your thresholds.
science.step2Description: We calculate your zones and create a profile of your strengths and weaknesses.
science.step3Description: Follow a program that knows exactly what pace you should hold today.

cta.title: Ready to take your running to the next level?
cta.description: Join today and get access to professional tools that help you reach your goals faster and safer.
cta.noCreditCard: No credit card required to start.

hub.title: Train smarter,
hub.description: The all-in-one platform for physiological testing, AI-powered training programs, and athlete management. Built for everyone who takes performance seriously.

athletes.title: For Athletes
athletes.hubDescription: AI-powered daily workouts, race predictions, and training zones based on your physiology.

hero.title: Train based on
hero.description: Get daily workouts adapted to your readiness, training zones calculated from your lactate tests, and race predictions based on real data — not guesswork.

painPoints.title: Sound familiar?
painPoints.item0: Following generic plans that don't match your fitness level
painPoints.item1: No idea if you're training at the right intensity
painPoints.item2: Guessing your race pace instead of knowing it
painPoints.item3: Training hard on days your body needs recovery

features.title: Everything you need to perform
features.description: Tools that elite athletes use — now available to you.

aiWod.title: AI Daily Workout
aiWod.description: A personalized workout every day, adjusted to your readiness, fatigue, and training plan.

readiness.title: Readiness & HRV
readiness.description: Daily health check that tells you whether to push hard or take it easy.

racePrediction.title: Race Prediction
racePrediction.description: See your predicted 10K, half marathon, and marathon times based on your actual data.

zones.title: Zone Calculation
zones.description: Exact training zones from lactate tests — heart rate, pace, and power.

progress.title: Progress Tracking
progress.description: Track your development over time with clear graphs and trend analysis.

sync.title: Strava & Garmin Sync
sync.description: Automatic import of workouts from your favorite devices and apps.

cta.title: Ready to train smarter?
cta.description: Sign up free and connect with your coach — or train on your own with AI-powered guidance.

coaches.title: For Coaches
coaches.hubDescription: AI studio, program builder, test reports, and athlete monitoring in one platform.

hero.title: Coach smarter with
hero.description: Build training programs, analyze lactate tests, monitor athlete load, and use AI to scale your coaching — all from one platform.

painPoints.title: Sound familiar?
painPoints.item0: Spending hours on spreadsheets instead of coaching
painPoints.item1: No way to objectively track athlete load and injury risk
painPoints.item2: Lactate test results stuck in PDF reports nobody reads

features.title: Your digital coaching toolkit
features.description: Everything you need to deliver world-class coaching at scale.

aiStudio.title: AI Studio
aiStudio.description: Generate programs, analyze data, and get coaching suggestions powered by AI.

programBuilder.title: Program Builder
programBuilder.description: Build periodized training programs with 4 proven methodologies.

testReports.title: Test Reports
testReports.description: Professional lactate test reports with threshold detection and zone calculation.

monitoring.title: Athlete Monitoring
monitoring.description: Track training load, ACWR, readiness, and injury risk across your roster.

multiSport.title: Multi-Sport Support
multiSport.description: 17 sports with sport-specific dashboards, testing protocols, and training zones.

portal.title: Athlete Portal
portal.description: Your athletes get their own app with WOD, calendar, logging, and progress tracking.

cta.title: Ready to scale your coaching?
cta.description: Start your free trial and see how the platform transforms your workflow.
cta.subText: 14-day free trial. No credit card required.

gyms.title: For Gyms & Studios
gyms.hubDescription: Team management, multi-coach access, custom branding, and testing as a revenue stream.

hero.title: Elevate your gym with
hero.description: Offer physiological testing as a premium service, manage multiple coaches, and give your members a world-class training experience.

painPoints.title: Sound familiar?
painPoints.item0: Members churn because they don't see measurable progress
painPoints.item1: No premium services to differentiate from competitors
painPoints.item2: Coaches work in silos with their own spreadsheets
painPoints.item3: Client data is lost when a coach leaves

features.title: Built for gym operations
features.description: Enterprise tools that grow with your business.

teamManagement.title: Team Management
teamManagement.description: Manage all your members, coaches, and staff from one central dashboard.

multiCoach.title: Multi-Coach Access
multiCoach.description: Multiple coaches work in the same system with role-based permissions.

branding.title: Custom Branding
branding.description: White-label reports and dashboards with your gym's logo and colors.

api.title: API Access

dataContinuity.title: Data Continuity
dataContinuity.description: Client data stays with your business — even when coaches move on.

revenue.title: Testing as Revenue

cta.title: Ready to stand out?

clubs.title: For Sports Clubs

trainingFocus.title: Training focus
trainingFocus.description: Based on your test results, we recommend focusing on the following areas:

cta.title: Want a tailored training program?
cta.description: Contact your coach for personal program planning based on these test results. An individually adapted program optimizes your training and helps you reach your goals faster.

paceZones.title: Practical pace zones
paceZones.description: Based on your threshold values, these are the recommended paces for different training types:
paceZones.note: These paces are calculated from your individual threshold values. Heart-rate zones provide more precise guidance during training, especially in variable conditions.

raceFuelingEstimate.title: Race fueling
raceFuelingEstimate.description: An estimate of carbohydrate needs at race-like intensity based on metabolic test data.

validation.needTarget: Enter expected time, or distance together with speed/pace.

buildUp.title: Gut training until race day
buildUp.description: Build from {start} to {target} g/h with one race-like session per week.

interpretation.title: Interpretation
interpretation.description: The body can burn more carbohydrates than the gut can absorb. The recommendation is therefore a practical intake target, while estimated carb demand shows the load on glycogen stores.

testScope.title: About this test
testScope.description: This test was completed without VO₂ measurement. Lactate and heart-rate measurements are sufficient for calculating training zones and threshold values. VO₂ measurement is only required for VO₂max assessment and running economy analysis.

postTest.title: Post-measurements (post-max lactate)
postTest.description: Peak lactate after maximum load gives an indication of anaerobic capacity

dmax.title: About the D-max method
dmax.description: D-max is a mathematical method that identifies the lactate threshold by finding the point with maximum distance from the baseline to the measured lactate curve. This is especially important for well-trained athletes with "flat" lactate curves, where the traditional 4 mmol/L method can overestimate the threshold and lead to overtraining. R² shows how well the polynomial curve fits your data ({fit} fit).

substrate.title: Substrate utilization
substrate.description: The chart shows the share of fat and carbohydrate combustion at each intensity. The crossover point is where carbohydrate combustion overtakes fat combustion — below this intensity, fat oxidation dominates, which is important for base training and endurance.

runningEconomy.title: Running economy
runningEconomy.description1: Running economy measures how efficiently your body uses oxygen while running. The value is given in ml O₂ per kg body weight per kilometer (ml/kg/km) and shows how much oxygen is required to run one kilometer at a given speed.
runningEconomy.description2: Lower value = better economy. A runner with good economy uses less energy at the same speed compared with a runner with poorer economy.
runningEconomy.improvementNote: Running economy can be improved through plyometric training, strength training for legs and core, and technical running drills focused on cadence optimization.

descriptions.reduceIntensity: Based on your readiness, intensity has been lowered for better recovery.
descriptions.reduceVolume: Based on your readiness, the workout duration has been reduced.
descriptions.easyDay: Based on your readiness, an easier workout is recommended instead.
descriptions.rest: Your readiness indicates that you need rest. Skip today's workout and recover.
descriptions.default: The workout has been adjusted based on your current readiness.

timing.eatWithinRecoveryWindow: Eat within 30-60 minutes after the workout

hydration.afterWorkout: Drink {amount}ml fluid after the workout and keep topping up through the afternoon

share.title: {count}-day streak
share.description: I am keeping my streak alive!

adHocWorkoutHeroCard.title: Workout registered
adHocWorkoutHeroCard.description: {workoutName} is now included in today's overview and your nutrition plan has been adjusted for the workout.

strategicRest.title: Strategic Rest
strategicRest.description: Quality sleep and good nutrition today power tomorrow's training.

activeRecovery.title: Active Recovery
activeRecovery.description: Light movement and stretching are recommended for optimal recovery.

recharge.title: Recharge
recharge.description: Focus on hydration and nutrient-dense food today.

mentalPrep.title: Mental Preparation
mentalPrep.description: Visualize your goals and upcoming training sessions.

smartTraining.title: Good moment for smart training
smartTraining.description: You do not have a scheduled workout today. Choose an effort that strengthens consistency without disrupting recovery.

keepRhythm.title: Keep the rhythm
keepRhythm.description: A calm, controlled activity can be effective even on days without a fixed schedule.

capacity.title: Capacity to use
capacity.description: Your status looks stable. If you want to train today, choose quality over random volume.

descriptions.swimming: Good sleep, good food, and calm mobility today improve the quality of your next swim.
descriptions.cycling: Load up with sleep, energy, and leg recovery so your next ride has the right pressure.
descriptions.triathlon: Recover systematically today so you can meet the next discipline with better quality.
descriptions.hyrox: Give your body a day to restore strength, engine, and grip before the next HYROX workout.
descriptions.functional: Recovery today creates better quality in your next session with strength, heart rate, and technique.
descriptions.strength: Good recovery today improves power development and quality in your next strength session.
descriptions.skiing: Quality sleep and good energy today create better pressure and technique in your next ski session.
descriptions.racket: Light recovery today improves timing, footwork, and quality in your next racket session.
descriptions.team: Recovery today improves explosiveness and quality in your next team training.

low.swimming: Prioritize sleep, fluids, and light mobility for shoulders and thoracic spine so you are fresh for your next swim.
low.cycling: Focus on leg and hip recovery plus energy intake so you are ready for the next quality ride.
low.triathlon: Let your body absorb today's load. Simple mobility and good energy help you come back stronger across all three disciplines.
low.functional: Recovery today improves quality in the next combination of strength and engine. Stick to light mobility and good food.
low.strength: Give your nervous system, muscles, and joints time to recover today so the next strength session can be done with quality.
low.skiing: Prioritize calm recovery, fuel, and mobility for hips, ankles, and upper body before the next ski session.
low.racket: Recover lower legs, shoulders, and trunk today so your next session with direction changes and shots has better quality.
low.team: Let tendons, muscles, and nervous system recover today so you can be quick and explosive in the next team session.
low.running: Give tendons, calves, and feet calm loading today so your next run has better quality.
low.default: Prioritize sleep, fluids, and light mobility today so your body has time to absorb the training.

normal.swimming: Use the rest day to reset shoulders, back, and energy so your next swim has better quality.
normal.cycling: The rest day helps your legs respond better to the next threshold, VO2, or endurance ride.
normal.triathlon: Recovery today strengthens your whole system across swimming, cycling, and running, making the next key session more valuable.
normal.hyrox: The rest day lets strength, engine, and durability build before the next HYROX workout.
normal.functional: Recovery today improves quality in your next session with strength, heart rate, and technique.
normal.strength: Muscles get stronger when you recover. A calm day today improves quality in the next lift.
normal.skiing: The rest day creates room for better technique and power development in the next ski or poling session.
normal.racket: Recovery today helps you be quicker in footwork and sharper in timing next session.
normal.team: The rest day improves explosiveness, decision-making, and durability for the next team training.
normal.running: Your muscles, tendons, and energy systems build capacity while you rest before the next run.
normal.default: Your muscles adapt and get stronger while you rest.

open.low: Your status points to low load today. Choose mobility, light circulation, or low-stress technique.

high.cycling: Good day for a short quality ride, cadence work, or a controlled threshold block.
high.swimming: Good day for technique sets, pace feel, or a short quality swim.
high.triathlon: Choose a clear discipline today, such as a short quality session or transition work with control.
high.functional: Good moment for technique under heart rate, station work, or a short strength-and-engine session.
high.strength: Good day for a focused strength session with quality in the main lifts or accessory work.
high.skiing: Good day for poling strength, technique intervals, or a short quality session on rollerskis/skierg.
high.racket: Good day for footwork, direction changes, and short intense sequences with strong quality.
high.team: Good day for accelerations, match-situation technique, or a short strength-and-power session.
high.running: Good day for strides, hill technique, or a short quality run if you want to keep rhythm.
high.default: Your status looks good for a short quality session, technique block, or focused strength.

normal.cycling: An easy endurance ride, trainer technique, or hip and ankle mobility fits well today.
normal.swimming: Focus on technique, body position, and calm aerobic volume if you want to train today.
normal.triathlon: A calm discipline in zone 1-2 or technique work gives the best effect without disrupting the whole plan.
normal.functional: Keep it simple today: light engine, mobility, and technically clean reps.
normal.strength: Technique sets, trunk stability, and light accessory strength fit better today than high load.
normal.skiing: Calm aerobic training and technique focus give the most value today.
normal.racket: Prioritize movement quality, lighter shot volume, and injury-prevention work today.
normal.team: Light footwork, mobility, and injury-prevention work fit better than extra high-intensity volume today.
normal.running: An easy jog, walk, or running drills gives more value than chasing extra load today.
normal.default: Low-intensity training, technique work, or mobility fits best today.

recentActivity.title: Latest workout: {type}
recentActivity.descriptionNoMetrics: Your latest registered workout was {type}. Use it as the starting point for today's decision.
recentActivity.descriptionWithMetrics: {metrics}. This gives a better reference for how the rest of the day should be managed.

hints.lowReadiness: Your body has already had load recently. Prioritize recovery, fluids, and low-intensity movement before adding more.
hints.highLoad: The latest workout was demanding. If you train again today, keep the next effort short, controlled, and complementary.
hints.default: Use the latest workout as your compass. If you choose to train again today, complement rather than duplicate the load.

recentTestsCard.title: Sport tests
recentTestsCard.description: Recent physical, hockey, and custom tests. Full view in the Tests tab.

clientLoadSummary.title: Load
clientLoadSummary.description: Acute:chronic workload (ACWR) — proxy for injury risk.

optimal.label: Optimal load
optimal.helper: ACWR between 0.8 and 1.3 — the adaptation sweet spot without elevated injury risk.

caution.label: Caution
caution.helper: ACWR 1.3-1.5 — monitor closely and avoid increasing volume further.

danger.label: High injury risk
danger.helper: ACWR 1.5-2.0 — reduce acute load over the next few days.

critical.label: Critical
critical.helper: ACWR > 2.0 — high injury risk. Plan a deload or rest.

detraining.label: Detraining
detraining.helper: ACWR < 0.8 — load is too low, ramp gradually.

unknown.label: Insufficient data
unknown.helper: No logged workouts in the last 30 days — ACWR needs at least 28 days of data.

fieldTest.title: Want more accurate zones?
fieldTest.description: A field test (like a 30-minute time trial or a lactate threshold test) provides much more accurate training zones. We recommend doing one within the first few weeks.
